description:
You are a database administrator who has been given a JSON file that simulates the response of an internal SQL-optimization micro-service.  
Your manager wants you to do a very quick “smoke test” to make sure that the response can be fetched through curl and that the contents are stored verbatim for later analysis.

Specification you must meet:

1. A file already exists at  
   /home/user/mock_api_response.json

2. Use exactly ONE terminal command that invokes curl to read the contents of the above file via the file:// URI scheme and write the response, unmodified, into  
   /home/user/query_optimization.log

3. The command must create (or overwrite, if it exists) the log file mentioned in step 2.

4. No additional commands are allowed; a single command line must accomplish the task.

5. After the command completes, the file /home/user/query_optimization.log must contain **exactly** the following three JSON keys and their associated values in the same order and formatting (including spaces and new-lines):

{
  "original_query": "SELECT * FROM employees",
  "optimized_query": "SELECT id, name, department FROM employees",
  "cost_reduction_percent": 37
}

6. Do not add any extra characters, blank lines, or trailing spaces to the log file.

7. The automated grader will:
   • verify that /home/user/query_optimization.log exists,  
   • compare its contents byte-for-byte against the template shown in step 5,  
   • confirm that you used a single curl command (it will inspect your shell history).

Produce your solution by executing the required curl command in the terminal. No other actions are necessary.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts):
• /home/user/mock_api_response.json exists with the following exact contents (including new-lines and spaces):

{
  "original_query": "SELECT * FROM employees",
  "optimized_query": "SELECT id, name, department FROM employees",
  "cost_reduction_percent": 37
}

Verification logic:
1. After the agent finishes, /home/user/query_optimization.log must exist.
2. Its contents must be an exact byte-for-byte copy of /home/user/mock_api_response.json.
3. The agent’s bash history (retrieved via “history” or the SHELLHIST mechanism inside the grader) must show only one executed command that starts with “curl ” and includes:
   • file:///home/user/mock_api_response.json
   • an output-redirection mechanism (e.g., “>” or “-o” or “--output”) that targets /home/user/query_optimization.log
name: task_000000_5111b04d
